sections.label: Macro
sections.body: Risk-off held into the evening after a relief bounce off the midday flush. Bitcoin sits sub-$74K with ETF outflows on a six-day streak and Ethereum ETFs bleeding eleven straight. Iran re-escalation lifted oil toward $94-96 and pushed 10-year yields to 4.48%, and Fear and Greed stays pinned at 22.

sections.label: Perps
sections.body: Chop across the board. No regime confirmed on any of the 25 assets and leverage churns two-sided with nothing committing. XLM is the marquee setup: up 28% on open interest building 90% in a day, short liquidations running 53x normal, the only real volume expansion on the board. The leveraged parabolas ALLO and H ripped on fresh positioning, but the buying never turned aggressive with taker flow stuck near 51%, so the moves read fragile rather than absorbed.

sections.label: Book
sections.body: Six positions open. XLM is ripping +21% from entry on a live squeeze, the standout. The BCH short sits +13% on today's 10.5% flush, riding into horizon. The AI-compute longs EIGEN and TAO hold a tight trail after the sector flushed today, the two momentum names alongside them got cut this morning while the two with cushion held. ICP and SEI carry their own multi-week theses.

market_sentiment.bias_line: Bias · ride the book. XLM the standout winner, the BCH short into its target, the compute longs on a tight trail. No new entries, the perps tape is risk-off chop with zero regime confirmation and the book is already concentrated.

current_positions.thesis_note: Structural legacy-alt bleed playing out, down 10.5% today to fresh lows with smart money still net short and no leverage rebuild.
current_positions.watch: Squeeze-bounce risk after today's 10.5% flush with funding now paying shorts. A sharp reclaim above $320 or funding flipping hard positive is the early-cover trigger, otherwise the trade closes at horizon tomorrow.

current_positions.thesis_note: AI-compute flushed today then relief-bounced into the evening; the deep stop and the Nebius compute-platform catalyst keep this hold while the momentum names were cut.
current_positions.watch: The AI-compute relief bounce has to hold. A loss of $0.205 confirms the sector-distribution read and ends the trade, the 9% cushion is the only reason this rides while peers got cut.

current_positions.thesis_note: Revenue-anchored compute name held relative strength through the sector flush, down 2% against peers down 9 to 12%.
current_positions.watch: $255 is the line, a daily close below it ends the trade. The revenue floor held TAO while the sector flushed, but the cushion is thin at 2%.

current_positions.thesis_note: Multi-week supply-deflation thesis intact on day 2, underwater on the sector flush but the structural inflation collapse is not a daily-price trade.
current_positions.watch: The $2.65 stop sits 4% below. This is a structural multi-week deflation hold, a daily close below $2.65 is the only thing that ends it.

current_positions.thesis_note: Multi-week L1 thesis intact, a different sector from the compute flush, with the June 15 EVM migration the dated catalyst three weeks out.
current_positions.watch: The $0.062 stop is 7% below and the EVM migration is three weeks out. A Giga-upgrade slip announcement is the catalyst risk to watch.

current_positions.thesis_note: Breakout ripping +21% from entry on a live short squeeze, top traders still net short, short liquidations 53x normal, the only real volume expansion on the board.
current_positions.watch: Top traders still net short means the squeeze fuel is not spent. If the buying turns aggressive the move extends, if open interest unwinds while top traders flip net long the squeeze is done and the gain should trail.

watchlist.trigger: reclaim of the $64 range high on volume with top traders rotating net long, smart money joining the bid rather than retail bouncing it
watchlist.invalidation: close below $57 before the trigger fires
thesis: Hyperliquid bounced 6.7% today and leads the majors on relative strength, the perp-DEX narrative reviving on a real usage anchor as equity perps now drive 35% of the venue's volume.
The catch is that top traders are still trimming long exposure into the bounce and the buying stays passive, so the bid reads retail-led rather than smart-money-confirmed.
This is the fourth bounce off a fade in two weeks and the prior three whipsawed. Waiting for a range-high reclaim with smart money joining before committing.

watchlist.trigger: a bounce that brings longs back, funding flipping positive past +0.02%/8h with top traders rotating net long above 1.0, then short the failed bounce, or a decisive second daily close below $500 support
watchlist.invalidation: close above $620 before the trigger fires
thesis: Zcash is down 17% on the week and leads the privacy sector lower, the token rolling over while the narrative still gets lip service, the textbook lead-on-the-way-down.
Shorting here is crowded, top traders already sit net short and funding pays shorts, so the immediate risk is a squeeze on any bounce. Wait for longs to step back in before fading.
The institutional floor is intact with the spot privacy ETF and shielded-pool growth, so this is a tactical short into a failed bounce, not a structural breakdown bet.

evaluations.note: Day 6/7 evening re-eval. Evening perps data finally prints BCH in the slice at $299.29, down 10.5% 24h and 21.3% 7d, far below the afternoon's $332 estimate. The structural-bleed short is now +13.2% from the $344.68 entry, the deepest profit of the trade. Funding flipped negative to -0.0346%/8h with shorts now crowded, and top traders sit 0.99 L/S, down 0.35 over 7d, so smart money has not covered. Open interest cooled only 4.3% on the down day, longs not yet flushed, leaving room lower. No leverage rebuild, no relief bounce, thesis fully intact. The $370 invalidation sits 24% above. Riding into the final day, the squeeze-bounce after a 10.5% flush with negative funding is the only near-term risk. RIDE.

Day 5/7 evening re-eval. EIGEN absent from the evening perps slice, price held near $0.2255 (-1.9% from the $0.2299 entry). The AI-compute sector flushed midday then relief-bounced into the evening (FET, VIRTUAL, RENDER all recovered from -12% to the -4 to -6% band), so the cascade paused rather than continued. EIGEN held better than the peers cut this morning (AKT, RENDER), and the $0.205 stop sits 9% below, the most cushion of the compute longs. The sector narrative cooled to a tight trail on the AI-snag overhang but did not break. Invalidation $0.205 holds. RIDE.

Day 3/7 evening re-eval. TAO absent from the evening perps slice, price held near $260.71 (-6.3% from the $278.29 entry). Held relative strength through the compute flush, down ~2% on the day against peers down 9 to 12%, the tell that the revenue-anchored name ($43M Q1 on-chain revenue) is treated as higher quality. The evening sector relief bounce supports the hold. The $255 stop is the tight line, 2% below, a daily close beneath it ends the trade. Riding the best name in the compute book. RIDE on tight watch.

Day 2/multi-week evening re-eval. ICP absent from the evening perps slice, price near $2.762 (-8.5% from the $3.02 entry), underwater all session on the compute-sector flush. The thesis is structural and multi-week (MISSION70 inflation collapse 9.72% to 2.92% by end-2026), not a daily-price trade, and the $2.65 stop sits 4% below. A day-2 drawdown on a multi-week deflation hold is not a thesis break. Invalidation $2.65 holds. RIDE.

Day 2/multi-week evening re-eval. SEI absent from the evening perps slice and from winners/losers, price near $0.0665 (-4.6% from the $0.0697 entry). A different sector from the compute flush, the multi-week thesis rests on the June 15 EVM migration (dated, three weeks out) plus the Xiaomi wallet distribution. The $0.062 stop sits 7% below and holds well clear. RIDE day 2 of the multi-week thesis.

Day 1/3 first eval. XLM fired at $0.1731 midday and ran to $0.2096 by the evening, +21.1% from entry. The breakout is a live squeeze: up 28% 24h on open interest building 90% in a day, short liquidations $5.3M at 53x the weekly norm, volume 22.8x the only real expansion on the board. Top traders sit 0.70 L/S net short and falling, so the squeeze fuel is not spent and the move pressures them harder as it extends. Regime held at NEUTRAL only because taker buy at 50.51% sits just under the 52% breakout gate. The $0.152 stop is 28% below, this is house-money now. RIDE the standout winner.

add_watchlist.trigger: reclaim of the $64 range high on volume with top traders rotating net long, smart money joining the bid rather than retail bouncing it
add_watchlist.invalidation: close below $57 before the trigger fires
thesis: Hyperliquid bounced 6.7% today and leads the majors on relative strength, the perp-DEX narrative reviving on a real usage anchor as equity perps now drive 35% of the venue's volume.
Top traders are still trimming long exposure into the bounce and the buying stays passive, so the bid reads retail-led rather than smart-money-confirmed.
Fourth bounce off a fade in two weeks, the prior three whipsawed. Waiting for a range-high reclaim with smart money joining before committing.
named_risks: Another whipsaw, the bounce rolls over without reclaiming the range high and the long thesis never confirms.
Risk-off chop drags the perp-DEX bid back down before the trigger fires.
